claim: Stripe's Minions system uses hybrid deterministic-agentic workflows: fixed graph of deterministic steps (linting, type checking, CI) interspersed with agentic reasoning nodes that generate code
actionable: Build AI coding workflows as blueprints alternating deterministic validation steps with agentic generation; enforce validation before agent iteration continues
source_title: Stripe's Coding Agents Ship 1,300 PRs EVERY Week

claim: Workflows (fixed graph with LLM at certain steps) are more deterministic than agent loops (where agent decides tool calls); for high-stakes systems, workflows are preferable
actionable: Use workflows for reliability-critical tasks (production code, large codebases); use agent loops for exploratory/low-stakes tasks where flexibility matters more
source_title: Stripe's Coding Agents Ship 1,300 PRs EVERY Week

claim: Context curation using MCP tools should happen deterministically at workflow start, selecting a relevant subset of available tools/documentation based on the request, not feeding all context upfront
actionable: Before handing off to agent, run deterministic context curation: extract relevant docs, pick 2-5 key tools from large tool sets, avoid overwhelming agent with all available tools
source_title: Stripe's Coding Agents Ship 1,300 PRs EVERY Week

claim: Deterministic validation steps (linting, type checking) save tokens and reduce LLM errors compared to having agents handle validation; compounds into system-wide reliability gains
actionable: Don't rely on agents to 'just follow the rules'; insert deterministic linting/testing steps that force failures early so agents can fix specific, unambiguous problems
source_title: Stripe's Coding Agents Ship 1,300 PRs EVERY Week

claim: Stripe runs isolated EC2 instances ('cattle, not pets') for each Minion execution with pre-loaded codebases and lint caching, enabling fast parallel execution and secure permissioning at scale
actionable: For high-throughput AI coding at enterprise scale, provision ephemeral cloud instances per agent run rather than trying to manage local worktrees; pre-warm with codebase and build cache
source_title: Stripe's Coding Agents Ship 1,300 PRs EVERY Week

claim: Selecting small subset of tests from 3+ million total tests to validate agent output: filters tests by relevance to change, runs them, escalates human review after max 2 agent retry loops
actionable: Don't run full test suite on generated code; select 50-200 tests most relevant to the changed files, set max retry count (2-3), escalate to human review after that
source_title: Stripe's Coding Agents Ship 1,300 PRs EVERY Week

claim: Shopify (Roast), Airbnb, and AWS all independently converged on the same hybrid deterministic-agentic workflow pattern, indicating this is the industry standard for reliable AI coding at scale
actionable: When designing AI coding workflows, adopt the deterministic-agentic hybrid pattern; don't expect end-to-end agent autonomy to work reliably for production code
source_title: Stripe's Coding Agents Ship 1,300 PRs EVERY Week
